Category: #  This file has two kinds of rows. Read this before editing:
#  │  LOCKED-NAME ROWS  (Campaign Setup + Base Operation sections)               │
#  │  The engine looks these up by their exact "Variable / Risk Event" name.     │
#  │  DO NOT rename them — the simulation will silently use defaults instead.    │
#  │  Risk rows are processed generically — their names are for display only.    │
#  │  SAFE TO ADD: Copy any risk row
Variable / Risk Event: paste at the end of the section

Category: #  │  SAFE TO REMOVE: Delete any risk row you do not want to model.              │
#  Category           : section label (Campaign Setup / Base Operation / Risk type)
#  Variable / Risk Event : row name. LOCKED for Campaign Setup + Base Operation rows.
#  Type               : do not change — defines how the row is processed:
#                         Random input = integer range (e.g. stages per well)
#                         Risk         = probability event with triangular delay
#  Probability        : 0–1 for Risk rows. N/A for Duration/Historical/Random input.
#  Min / Most Likely / Max Days : triangular distribution bounds (days).
#                         [*] Historical rows show reference values only —
#                             actual values are resampled from historical_wells.csv.
#  Simulation Impact  : free-text description (not parsed by engine — edit freely).
#                         campaign = single draw for whole campaign (crew absence
#  CONSEQUENCE OVERRIDE COLUMNS (last 5 columns — Risk rows only
#  Leave blank to use the built-in library defaults. Fill in to override for one risk.
#    extra_wireline_runs  : additional wireline trips triggered by this event
#    Stage-scope:  If last campaign had 180 stages and 12 screenouts → p = 12/180 = 0.067
#    Campaign-scope: "30% chance of a wireline crew issue this campaign" → p = 0.30
#  CAMPAIGN SETUP  [LOCKED NAMES — change Probability/Min/ML/Max values only]

Simulation Impact: Defines number of pads — change Min/Max to match your campaign size
Stages planned per well — change Min/Max to match your well design
Stages receiving a temperature survey — set to 0 if not applicable
Probability cement evaluation runs offline (0 = always online, 1 = always offline). Default 0.8.

Category: #  BASE OPERATIONS  [LOCKED NAMES — change Min / Most Likely / Max Days only]
#  Duration rows: triangular distribution sampled per well per iteration.
#  Historical rows: resampled bootstrap from historical_wells.csv — Min/ML/Max
#                   shown here are reference values only and are NOT used at runtime.

Simulation Impact: CT hours for cement evaluation (only charged when running online) — adjust Min/ML/Max to your typical run time
CT hours for pre-frac wellbore cleanup — set all three to 0 if you do not run a scraper
[REFERENCE ONLY — resampled from FracDaysPerStage in historical_wells.csv at runtime]
Wireline time per temperature survey stage — set to 0 if no temperature logging
Wireline time to set one isolation plug before next perforation run
Wireline time to perforate one stage
Wireline time to set the final-stage isolation plug
[REFERENCE ONLY — resampled from MillingDaysPerPlug in historical_wells.csv at runtime]

Category: #  Scope = stage: probability applies per individual stage.

Simulation Impact: Possible contingency plug required
Screen out event; possible extra stage, CT intervention and remedial treatment
Extra wireline run required to re-perforate
Guns must be re-run
Additional CCL/UPCT pass required
Additional CT cleanout required before perforation
Remedial CT intervention
Additional plug to set and mill; testing required
Surface equipment troubleshooting
Repair and retest required; affects whole well not just a stage

Category: #  ← ADD NEW TECHNICAL RISK ROWS HERE if needed
#  Scope = campaign: single Bernoulli draw for the whole campaign.
#  Models crew absences and equipment downtime that affect all concurrent
#  operations — NOT each well independently. Prevents the structural error of
#  treating one crew walkout as 30 independent per-well events.

Simulation Impact: Waiting on replacement crew; all wireline ops delayed
Delayed cleanout and contingency response
Milling backlog develops
Reduced pumping rate or frac pause
Temperature logs delayed
Material logistics delay
Perforation cannot proceed until resolved

Category: #  ← ADD NEW RESOURCE RISK ROWS HERE if needed
#  Scope = campaign: single event affecting the whole operation.

Simulation Impact: Operations suspended pending regulatory action
Crew or equipment movement affected
Operations paused due to weather
Work suspended pending security clearance
Crew rotation severely disrupted
Crew rotation delay due to accommodation shortage
Specialist service not available on schedule
